record.population: Patients with sepsis in the triage area of the emergency department
record.predictor: NEWS, SOS, qSOFA, SIRS, ESI triage
record.outcome: 28-day mortality, 28-day mechanical ventilator usage, 28-day vasopressor usage
record.method: Retrospective single-center study, ROC analysis, Sensitivity and Specificity analysis
record.effect_size: NEWS vs Non-survivor median score: 8 (IQR 6, 10) vs Survivor: 5 (IQR 3, 7), p < 0.001; SOS median: 6 (4, 7) vs 3 (2, 5), p < 0.001
record.mortality_rate: 12.36% (68/550 total; 6 deaths in ED, 23 in palliative care)
record.sofa_score: qSOFA median (IQR): All 1 (1, 2), Survivor 1 (1, 2), Non-survivor 2 (1, 2), p < 0.001
record.mechanical_ventilation: 28-day intubation free day (mean): Survivor 28 (2.2), Non-survivor 4 (3.1), p < 0.001
record.hospital_los: Median (IQR): All 68 (11, 233) hours, Survivor 56 (11, 199) hours, Non-survivor 142 (63, 342) hours, p = 0.045
record.vasopressor_use: 28-day vasopressor free day (mean): Survivor 28 (2.0), Non-survivor 6 (2.4), p < 0.001
record.auc_value: 28-day mortality: NEWS 0.770, SOS 0.750, qSOFA 0.747; 28-day MV: NEWS 0.750, SOS 0.751; 28-day Vasopressor: NEWS 0.763, SOS 0.755
record.notes: Data extracted from Tables 1, 2, and 3, and Figures 3 and 4 across pages 6-8. NEWS demonstrated the highest AUROC for mortality prediction. Hospital LOS is reported in hours. Secondary outcomes include intubation-free and vasopressor-free days.

record.population: Patients with sepsis in the triage area of the Emergency Department (ED)
record.predictor: NEWS (National Early Warning Score), SOS (Search Out Severity Score), qSOFA (quick Sequential Organ Failure Assessment), ESI triage (Emergency Severity Index), SIRS (Systemic Inflammatory Response syndrome)
record.outcome: 28-day mortality, 28-day mechanical ventilator usage, 28-day vasopressor usage
record.method: Retrospective, single-center study; ROC analysis (AUROC), sensitivity and specificity analysis
record.performance: AUROC for 28-day mortality: NEWS 0.770 (0.705-0.835), SOS 0.750 (0.685-0.815), qSOFA 0.747 (0.688-0.806), ESI 0.599, SIRS 0.588. NEWS cut-off ≥4: sensitivity 91.30%, specificity 28.69%. NEWS cut-off ≥5: sensitivity 86.96%, specificity 45.74%.
record.mechanical_ventilation: AUROC for 28-day usage: NEWS 0.750, SOS 0.751, qSOFA 0.734, ESI 0.642, SIRS 0.581
record.vasopressor_use: AUROC for 28-day usage: NEWS 0.763, SOS 0.755, qSOFA 0.741, ESI 0.624, SIRS 0.579
record.notes: Data extracted from Tables 2 and 3 (page 7) and Figures 3 and 4 (page 8). The study concludes NEWS outperforms other scores in the ED triage setting for predicting sepsis outcomes. Demographic and baseline clinical characteristics (age, lactate, etc.) were not present on the included pages 7-9.

record.population: Patients with suspicion of sepsis admitted to the Emergency Department (ED) of a tertiary referral hospital in Brazil.
record.predictor: qSOFA score, Severity Criteria (GCS ≤ 14, SBP ≤ 100 mmHg, RR ≥ 22 breaths/min), age, lactate, time to antibiotic (ATB), positive blood cultures, vasopressor use at ED.
record.outcome: 30-day mortality, overall mortality, ICU admission, and overall length of stay (LOS).
record.timing: At ED admission/triage; outcomes measured at 30 days and during hospital stay.
record.method: Retrospective cohort study; Univariate and Multivariate regression analysis (Relative Risk); ROC curve analysis.
record.effect_size: 30-day mortality (Multivariate): Vasopressors at ED RR 2.14 (95% CI 1.44–3.17), Positive blood cultures RR 1.63 (95% CI 1.10–2.41). ICU admission (Multivariate): qSOFA=1 RR 1.59, qSOFA=2 RR 1.54, qSOFA=3 RR 1.54, Vasopressors at ED RR 2.50 (95% CI 2.20–2.84).
record.performance: qSOFA for 30-day mortality: AUC=0.58; Severity criteria for 30-day mortality: AUC=0.52.
record.hospital_los: Overall length of stay Median [IQR]: 10 [6–17] days.
record.notes: Evidence extracted from text, Table 1 (Characteristics), Table 3 (Mortality predictors), Table 4 (LOS/ICU predictors), and Figure 2 (ROC curves). The study concludes qSOFA and severity criteria are poor predictors of mortality but qSOFA is a valuable predictor for ICU admission.

record.population: Adult patients (>18 years) presenting to the emergency department (ED) of a tertiary-care university hospital with an initial diagnosis of sepsis or infection-related conditions and treated with a sepsis protocol.
record.predictor: National Early Warning Score (NEWS), quick Sequential Organ Failure Assessment (qSOFA), Search Out Severity (SOS) score, Emergency Severity Index (ESI) triage, and Systemic Inflammatory Response System (SIRS) criteria
record.outcome: Primary: 28-day mortality; Secondary: 28-day mechanical ventilator usage and 28-day vasopressor usage
record.timing: Predictors measured at ED triage; outcomes measured at 28 days
record.method: Retrospective cross-sectional study; ROC analysis (AUROC) with 95% CI
record.performance: NEWS provided the highest AUROC for 28-day mortality (0.770) and outperformed other scores for mechanical ventilator and vasopressor usage prediction.
record.sensitivity: Reported in Table 3 (not fully visible in text, but NEWS AUROC is 0.770)
record.specificity: Reported in Table 3 (not fully visible in text, but NEWS AUROC is 0.770)
record.notes: Evidence extracted from abstract (page 2), methods (page 3), results text, flow chart (Figure 1), and ROC curve (Figure 2) on page 4. The study concludes NEWS is the most accurate triage tool for predicting 28-day mortality in sepsis patients.

record.study: Seymour et al. 2016 (Sepsis-3 Clinical Criteria Assessment)
record.population: Patients with suspected infection in ICU and non-ICU settings across 5 US and non-US data sets (UPMC, KPNC, VA, ALERTS, KCEMS)
record.predictor: SOFA score, qSOFA (quick SOFA), SIRS criteria, LODS score
record.outcome: In-hospital mortality; Secondary: In-hospital mortality or ICU length of stay ≥3 days
record.timing: Predictors measured near onset of suspected infection (from 48 hours before to 24 hours after); Outcome: In-hospital
record.method: Retrospective and prospective cohort studies; Multivariable logistic regression; AUROC analysis; Bayesian information criterion for qSOFA development
record.effect_size: 10-fold variation in mortality across deciles of baseline risk (0.7% to 8%); 3- to 11-fold increase in mortality for SOFA ≥2 vs <2 in ICU; up to 80-fold change for SOFA ≥2 vs <2 in non-ICU
record.performance: In ICU: SOFA AUROC=0.74 (95% CI, 0.73-0.76), LODS AUROC=0.75 (95% CI, 0.73-0.76), SIRS AUROC=0.64 (95% CI, 0.62-0.66). Outside ICU: qSOFA AUROC=0.70 (95% CI, 0.68-0.71)
record.mortality_rate: UPMC: 4%; KPNC: 5%; VA: 6%; ALERTS: 18%; KCEMS: 11%; ICU encounters (UPMC): 16-17%; Non-ICU encounters (UPMC): 3%
record.sofa_score: ICU encounters: Mean 6.2-6.3 (SD 3.9-4.0), Median 6 (IQR 3-9); Non-ICU encounters: Mean 1.4 (SD 1.9-2.0), Median 1 (IQR 0-2)
record.age: All encounters: Mean 61 years (SD 19); ICU: 62 (17); Non-ICU: 60-61 (20)
record.lactate: Measured in 57% of KPNC encounters; ≥2.0 mmol/L in 20-21% of ICU encounters and 2% of non-ICU encounters
record.icu_los: Secondary outcome defined as ICU LOS ≥3 days
record.hospital_los: Median 6 days (IQR 3-10) for all encounters; ICU: 12 days (7-19/20); Non-ICU: 6 days (3-9)
record.platelets: Included in SOFA and LODS (Table 1); Absent in 15% of non-ICU encounters
record.bilirubin: Included in SOFA and LODS (Table 1); Absent in 62% of non-ICU encounters
record.notes: Data extracted from Tables 1, 2, and 3, and Results text on pages 4-6. Performance metrics (AUROC) and score distributions (Figure 2) compare ICU vs. non-ICU settings. qSOFA was developed as a simpler bedside tool.
